mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic output, which directly boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations. A positive monthly change in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as the market prices in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: The predictive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is less than 0.05, or the average return following positive INDPRO changes is not statistically greater than zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate from an elevated level indicates improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer spending and confidence, leading to higher equity prices. When UNRATE falls below the 9% threshold after being above 10%, this disinflation of labor slack signals positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return after trigger events is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer price inflation and a stagnant federal funds rate compress real interest rates, which initially boosts equities but eventually signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening. The joint deviation of CPIAUCSL up and FEDFUNDS flat/down predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market re-prices tightening risk.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional rank of the interaction term over rolling 2-year windows yields a Rank IC below -0.03, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: During the expansionary phase of the business cycle (GDPC1 above its previous cyclical peak), continued strong real GDP growth reinforces corporate earnings momentum and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The signal is active only in the expansion regime.
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 exceeds its prior peak, the 60-day forward SPX return is non-positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, a primary driver of corporate revenues. When RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 1%, it predicts positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reacts to fresh demand data.
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a retail sales growth trigger is not greater than the unconditional average return, or the hit rate is below 50% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (UNRATE below 4%), further declines in unemployment signal wage inflation pressures that erode corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The negative signal is regime-conditional on a tight labor market.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 4%, the correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 20-day returns is not negative, or the signal's rank IC is above 0.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, both of which are negative for equity valuations. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 3%, it triggers a negative outlook for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day return after inflation crosses above 3% is not negative, or the signal fails to predict direction better than a random classifier over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer durable goods demand. A rising trend in HOUST signals expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings and equity prices over a quarterly horizon. This always-on trend signal uses positive monthly changes in HOUST to predict positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The predictive Rank IC of HOUST changes for 60-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over a 5-year walk-forward period, or the signal's average return is not significantly different from zero.
